title: Bitcoin miner Riot Platforms reports record $211M Q1 net income
text: Riot Platforms reported a $211.8-million net income for Q1 2024 — a company record and a 1,000% increase from the same time last year. This quarter, Riot reported net income of $211.8 million and earnings per share of $0.82 which are new record highs for quarterly results. Riot's first quarter results show mining revenue spiked 55.4% year-on-year to $74.6 million, primarily driven by a 131% increase in Bitcoin's price. However, the firm's total revenue was $79.3 million, falling 14% short of estimates by research firm Zacks. Despite the strong profitability, Riot faced rising costs with the miner saying a rise in net income and mining revenue was partially slowed by lower Bitcoin production and higher mining costs due to an increase in Bitcoin's network difficulty and hash rate. Specifically, the average cost to mine Bitcoin was $23,034 in the quarter, as compared to $9,438 per Bitcoin for the same three-month period in 2023. Riot currently boasts the third-largest hash rate among miners, trailing only rivals Marathon Digital and Core Scientific at 24.7 EH/s and 16.9 EH/s, respectively.
